steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined portion. The underlined sentence begins with ‘But,’ signaling a shift from criticism to a counter-argument that defends the books’ structure.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It presents a reason most critics think the Aubrey/Maturin series should have the literary renown of similar works like *The Forsyte Saga*.: The underlined portion says some critics argue the structure is valid — it says nothing about the series deserving renown, and ‘most critics’ is not supported. ✗
  • Choice B — It summarizes a positive assessment of a particular characteristic of the Aubrey/Maturin novels.: Other critics defend the structure (a characteristic) by viewing the series as one long work — a positive assessment. ✓
  • Choice C — It explains why many critics find the Aubrey/Maturin novels to be entertaining despite flaws in the novels' structures.: The passage is about whether the structure is a flaw, not about entertainment. ✗
  • Choice D — It argues that the unusual structure that O'Brian uses for *HMS Surprise* makes it one of his most entertaining books.: The underlined portion addresses the whole series, not just HMS Surprise, and does not mention entertainment. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
